message:
feat(resolv): implement RFC 1876 LOC RR codec [bd-58e87f]

Replaces the always-NULL/0 stubs with full encode/decode:

  __loc_aton  — parses "<lat-d> <lat-m> <lat-s>[.<frac>] <N|S>
                <lon-d> <lon-m> <lon-s>[.<frac>] <E|W>
                <alt>[m] [<size>[m] [<hp>[m] [<vp>[m]]]]" into
                the 16-byte binary form, returning 16 on success
                (matching glibc's binary-size return value).
  __loc_ntoa  — formats the 16-byte binary as ASCII, writing into
                the caller-provided buffer or a thread-local static.
                Outputs "DD MM SS.SSS NS DDD MM SS.SSS EW
                AA.AAm SS.SSm HH.HHm VV.VVm" matching libresolv,
                with the "; error: unknown LOC RR version"
                fallback for non-zero version bytes.

Mantissa-exponent precsize encoding (1 cm × 10^N) and the
2^31/100,000m biases all match the reference implementation.

Adds a 12-test conformance harness diff'd against glibc libresolv:
  - 5 ntoa cases (N+W, S+E with negative altitude, equator/PM
    zero point, custom precision overrides, unknown-version
    error path)
  - 5 aton cases (basic text, sub-second precision with custom
    precs, minutes-only-no-seconds, garbage rejection, NULL
    hardening)
  - aton/ntoa round-trip across 4 distinct coordinates
  - independent NULL-input hardening test for fl

All cases pass with zero divergences against glibc 2.42.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
